report_name: Medusa Ransomware Group Strikes Within 24 Hours of Breach, Microsoft Warns

threat_actors.name: Storm-1175
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated cybercrime group that deploys Medusa ransomware. The group is known for its high operational tempo, exploiting N-day and zero-day vulnerabilities to achieve ransomware deployment within 24-48 hours of initial access.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data exfiltration and encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Storm-1175 gains initial access by exploiting known vulnerabilities in public-facing assets, including Microsoft Exchange, GoAnywhere MFT, and SmarterMail, often deploying a web shell for persistence.

attack_patterns.name: Create Account
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor creates new user accounts on compromised systems for persistence.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Defenses
attack_patterns.description: The group tampers with or disables security solutions to evade detection during their operations.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: Storm-1175 uses legitimate remote management software such as ConnectWise ScreenConnect and AnyDesk for persistence and lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of the attack involves deploying Medusa ransomware to encrypt files on the victim's network.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Sensitive data is exfiltrated to cloud storage, often facilitated by the use of Cloudflare tunnels.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: The actor uses PowerShell as part of its Living Off the Land (LOTL) strategy for post-compromise activities.

attack_patterns.name: Service Execution
attack_patterns.description: The actor uses tools like PsExec for lateral movement within the compromised network.

malware.name: Medusa
malware.description: A ransomware payload deployed by the Storm-1175 group to encrypt victim files as part of a double extortion scheme.

tools.name: ConnectWise ScreenConnect
tools.description: Used by Storm-1175 for persistence and lateral movement after gaining initial access.

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool abused by Storm-1175 for persistence and lateral movement.

tools.name: SimpleHelp
tools.description: A legitimate remote management software used by Storm-1175 for post-compromise activities.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Used by the threat actor for lateral movement within the victim network.

tools.name: Cloudflare Tunnel
tools.description: Used by Storm-1175 to exfiltrate sensitive data to cloud storage.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2023-21529
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange exploited by Storm-1175 for initial access.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Microsoft Exchange Server Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-10035
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in GoAnywhere MFT exploited by Storm-1175 for initial access.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Fortra GoAnywhere MFT Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-23760
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in SmarterMail exploited by Storm-1175 for initial access.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: SmarterTools SmarterMail Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel Vulnerability

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2023-27350
vulnerabilities.description: One of over 16 distinct CVEs exploited by Storm-1175 since 2023 for rapid initial access.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: PaperCut MF/NG Improper Access Control Vulnerability

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2023-27351
vulnerabilities.description: One of over 16 distinct CVEs exploited by Storm-1175 since 2023 for rapid initial access.

identities.name: Healthcare organizations
identities.description: A primary target sector for Storm-1175's Medusa ransomware attacks, leading to disruption of patient care.

identities.name: Education organizations
identities.description: A primary target sector for Storm-1175, resulting in interruption of academic activities.

identities.name: Professional Services organizations
identities.description: A target sector for Storm-1175, leading to financial loss and reputational damage.

identities.name: Financial organizations
identities.description: A target sector for Storm-1175, leading to significant financial loss.

infrastructure.name: Web Shell Persistence
infrastructure.description: A web shell deployed by Storm-1175 on compromised public-facing assets for initial persistence.

infrastructure.name: Cloudflare Tunnel Exfiltration Channel
infrastructure.description: Cloudflare tunnels used by Storm-1175 to exfiltrate sensitive data to attacker-controlled cloud storage.

indicators.name: Medusa Actor Tooling Process Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Medusa Actor Tooling Process Execution
id: 2b1c8f9a-3d4e-4f5a-b6c7-d8e9f0a1b2c3
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of tools and malware commonly used by the Storm-1175 (Medusa) group.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/04/06/storm-1175-focuses-gaze-on-vulnerable-web-facing-assets-in-high-tempo-medusa-ransomware-operations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.t1570
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
      - '\ScreenConnect.Client.exe'
      - '\cloudflared.exe'
      - '\medusa.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of remote access tools and the Medusa ransomware binary used by Storm-1175.

indicators.name: AnyDesk Software Installation Registry Key
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: AnyDesk Software Installation Registry Key
id: 8e7d6c5b-4a3b-4c2d-b1a0-9f8e7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a registry key associated with AnyDesk, a tool abused by Storm-1175. This may indicate legitimate use.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/04/06/storm-1175-focuses-gaze-on-vulnerable-web-facing-assets-in-high-tempo-medusa-ransomware-operations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate installation of AnyDesk.
tags:
  - attack.t1219
level: low
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|endswith: '\Software\AnyDesk'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a registry key associated with AnyDesk, which could indicate its installation as part of a Storm-1175 intrusion.

indicators.name: Cloudflared Tunnel for C2 or Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Cloudflared Tunnel for C2 or Exfiltration
id: 5a4b3c2d-1e0f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of cloudflared.exe to create a tunnel, a technique used by Storm-1175 for C2 and data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/04/06/storm-1175-focuses-gaze-on-vulnerable-web-facing-assets-in-high-tempo-medusa-ransomware-operations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.t1090
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\cloudflared.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'tunnel'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command-line pattern for creating a Cloudflare tunnel, used by Storm-1175 for data exfiltration.

relationships.source_name: Cloudflared Tunnel for C2 or Exfiltration
